headlines.title: China Cuts Saudi Crude Orders as Hormuz Risks and Discounts Reshape Trade
headlines.summary: Some Chinese refiners have not nominated term crude cargoes from Saudi Arabia for August, while others have not been allocated any term supply for next month, as weak demand in China, competition from other producers, and continued disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz have slashed

headlines.title: Brent Surges Above $86 After Iran Strikes Two UAE Tankers in Hormuz
headlines.summary: Brent Crude prices soared by 3% early on Tuesday to above $86 per barrel, following an Iranian attack on two tankers of the United Arab Emirates in the southern lane of the Strait of Hormuz, in one of the biggest attacks in the recent re-escalation in the region. In early trading

headlines.title: Iran Sneaks 12 Million Barrels Past Renewed U.S. Oil Blockade
headlines.summary: Iran is estimated to have sneaked supertankers with a total of 12 million barrels of crude oil in the week between the end of the U.S. waiver on Iranian oil sales on July 7 and July 14, just as the U.S. announced the renewed blockade on Iranian ports and oil cargoes. Last Tuesday

headlines.title: China’s Crude Oil Imports Crash to Decade Low as Hormuz Crisis Bites
headlines.summary: China’s crude oil imports crashed to a decade-low in June as the reduced flows through the Strait of Hormuz hiked oil prices and reduced refiners’ appetite for costly crude. Overall Chinese imports of crude oil plunged by 41.3% in June from a year earlier, to just 29.27 million t

headlines.title: Oil Prices Have Jumped 12% Since Friday as War Risks Return
headlines.summary: Oil prices extended Monday’s surge into Asian trade on Tuesday, with Brent Crude prices hitting a one-month high, as the renewed war risk premium crashed the calmer trade of the past weeks amid renewed U.S.-Iran hostilities and the reinstated U.S. blockade of Iranian oil exports.

headlines.title: Oil Refiners Are Cashing In on a Market That Won’t Stay Broken
headlines.summary: Oil refiners have stumbled into one of the best profit environments in years. Crude prices have fallen back to where they traded before the Iran war erupted. But gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel remain stubbornly expensive. That combination has pushed refining margins to extraordin

headlines.title: America Extends Its Lead Over Russia and Saudi Arabia in Oil Output
headlines.summary: While the United States has been the world’s biggest consumer of oil for decades, it became the largest oil producer, too, back in 2018, and has retained that crown ever since. headlines.title: OPEC Cuts Demand Forecast Again as the Oil Market Starts Looking Past Hormuz
headlines.summary: OPEC has trimmed its 2026 global oil demand growth forecast for the third straight month, even as crude production rebounds across the Gulf and tanker traffic slowly returns to the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: Oil Prices Surge 8% After Trump Reimposes Iran Blockade
headlines.summary: Oil prices jumped more than 8% on Monday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced the United States would reinstate its blockade on Iran, forcing traders to once again price in the risk of prolonged disruption to energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz.
